title: Composition mechanism selection at framework-parity — per-case benchmark & the budgeted-host-node principle (prior-art survey)
summary: Grounds decision #1963 (strict per-case composition-mechanism rubric judged against a framework-parity, zero-compromise bar). Benchmarks each composition case against React/Vue/Svelte/Solid/Angular/Lit and against the FUI tree, and surveys the emerging standards bearing on the hardest case (deep structural composition). THE SPINE: a framework component is a function/object in a virtual tree — only leaf elements reify as DOM, so 10 providers = 0 nodes; a custom element is DEFINED as a DOM element (customElements.define binds behaviour to a tag = a node with a box, event-path slot, AX entry, :host). Slotting, Shadow-DOM scoping, connectedCallback lifecycle, ElementInternals semantics and focus are ALL keyed off that node — you cannot have them and not have the node. So the platform charges per node for a reason and NO emerging standard removes it (display:contents removes the box not the node; subgrid aligns through a wrapper; moveBefore makes reparenting non-destructive but doesn't reduce node count; DOM Parts ChildNodePart is proposal-stage since 2017 and addresses CONTENT regions not CE hosts; ElementInternals makes the node worth paying for, not free). The pattern that DOES reach zero-node parity — Haunted virtual(), Lit directives, Stencil functional components — does so by ABANDONING the registered host (a function that renders into the parent). Conclusion: framework parity for WE is a DISCIPLINE not a missing feature — BUDGET THE HOST NODE: pay it only for slot/shadow/AX-identity/lifecycle; use zero-node mechanisms (JSX functional components, comment-anchor directives, mixins/behaviors, display:contents) for everything else, exactly as frameworks compile structural layers away. Per-case verdicts: cases 2 (grouped/reactive), 4/5 (if/for/switch via comment anchors — all of Vue/Svelte/Solid/Lit converge here), 7 (behavioural composition via mixins/controllers) are AT PARITY on the raw platform today; case 1 (real-button) reaches full parity via emit-to-native (transient) — what React/Svelte/Solid do; cases 3/6/9 reach parity via display:contents / logical-portal modulo a small AX/fallback confirm; case 8 (is= on an EXISTING native element) has NO zero-compromise cross-browser path (Safari permanently refuses is= per WebKit #97; ElementInternals only helps your OWN autonomous element) — resolve via is=-as-progressive-enhancement + autonomous-element+ElementInternals, never load-bearing; case 10 (deep structural composition) is the contested one — the discipline view says it is solved today by zero-node function/directive render (not an invent-case), the more pessimistic view says HTML-declarative true-zero-node deep nesting needs an invented primitive (or generalizing the transient-to-comments bridge) — DOM Parts is a watch, not a blocker. Full grounding + citations in reports/2026-06-29-composition-framework-parity.md; catalog companion /research/dom-less-composition.
